title: schoenoplectus litoralis subsp. litoralis
intro: Schoenoplectus litoralis subsp. litoralis, also known as common cordgrass or salt-marsh cordgrass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the cattail family (Typhaceae) and is native to the coastal regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Schoenoplectus litoralis subsp. litoralis include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's stems and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
